reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: An attacker can trigger this via P2P network by sending a valid DataColumnSidecar followed by a forged DataColumnSidecar with the same inclusion proof and header but malicious KzgCommitments, causing cache hit and bypassing Merkle verification to accept invalid commitments. Severity adjusted from Critical to High because this is a single-client vulnerability affecting Prysm (31% of consensus layer validators), below the 33% threshold for Critical/High but above Medium threshold given the severity of accepting invalid cryptographic commitments.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: SG-004: Any cached/optimized inclusion proof verification must match full Merkle branch verification.
